title: Flat Tire
description: The lead truck hits a sharp rock. Hissing air signals another flat.

options.label: Change it yourself (2 hours)
options.outcome: The crew changes the tire in the rain. Back on the road, but behind schedule.

options.label: Use the spare and continue
options.outcome: Quick swap and you're moving again. Hope there are no more surprises.

options.label: Wait for help
options.outcome: Another crew passes by and helps. Cost you half a day.

title: Engine Trouble
description: The skidder coughs and sputters. Smoke rises from the engine compartment.

options.label: Field repair with spare parts
options.outcome: Your mechanic works through the problem. Fixed, but parts are depleted.

options.label: Limp to the next block slowly
options.outcome: Running on half power, you make it to a spot where proper repairs are possible.

options.label: Call for tow truck ($500)
options.outcome: The machine gets hauled out. Expensive, but the right choice.

title: Chainsaw Breakdown
description: The primary saw throws its chain into a stump. Something's bent.

options.label: Swap to backup saw
options.outcome: The backup saw is slower but works. Production drops for the day.

options.label: Send runner for parts
options.outcome: Someone drives to town. Lost time but the good saw runs again.

options.label: Try to straighten the bar
options.outcome: With patience and a hammer, it's serviceable. Probably.

title: Truck Stuck in Mud
description: The truck sinks to its axles in soft ground. It's not going anywhere without help.

options.label: Winch it out with the skidder
options.outcome: Slow work, but eventually free. Equipment takes a beating.

options.label: Dig and lay brush road
options.outcome: Everyone grabs shovels. Exhausting work but it works.

options.label: Leave it and walk out
options.outcome: You hike back with essential gear. The truck waits for drier weather.

title: Sudden Storm
description: Dark clouds roll in fast. Lightning flashes over the ridge.

options.label: Take shelter immediately
options.outcome: Everyone piles into vehicles. Safe but no work gets done.

options.label: Push through to the next block
options.outcome: You race the storm. Most make it, but conditions are dangerous.

options.label: Set up emergency camp
options.outcome: Tarps go up fast. You wait out the worst. Morale holds.

title: Flash Flood Warning
description: The creek you crossed this morning is now a torrent. Water's rising fast.

options.label: Move to high ground immediately
options.outcome: Everyone scrambles uphill. Equipment is safe but the day is lost.

options.label: Risk crossing now before it gets worse
options.outcome: A gamble. The trucks make it across, barely.

options.label: Camp on this side overnight
options.outcome: You wait for waters to drop. Lost a day but everyone's dry.

title: Extreme Cold Snap
description: Temperature drops to -30C overnight. Everything is frozen solid.

options.label: Keep vehicles running all night
options.outcome: Engines stay warm but fuel tanks drain fast.

options.label: Bundle up and wait it out
options.outcome: Everyone huddles in camp. A few frostbite scares but manageable.

options.label: Light a big warming fire
options.outcome: The fire keeps spirits high. Extra food consumed for energy.

title: Bear in Camp
description: A black bear has found the food cache. It's not happy about being interrupted.

options.label: Make noise and back away slowly
options.outcome: The bear eventually wanders off. Some food is ruined.

options.label: Use bear spray
options.outcome: Direct hit. The bear runs. Everyone's shaken but safe.

options.label: Fire a warning shot
options.outcome: The blast startles the bear into the bush. Compliance might ask questions.

title: Grizzly Territory
description: Fresh grizzly signs everywhere. Tracks, scat, claw marks on trees. This is her home.

options.label: Post bear watch and continue
options.outcome: Work continues with nervous glances. Productivity suffers.

options.label: Relocate operations for the day
options.outcome: You move to a different area. Safe but inefficient.

options.label: Report and request guidance
options.outcome: Wildlife officers advise caution. A two-day delay while they assess.

title: Moose on the Road
description: A bull moose stands in the middle of the road. He's not moving.

options.label: Wait him out
options.outcome: After 20 minutes of staring, he finally ambles off.

options.label: Honk and inch forward
options.outcome: The moose charges the truck! Minor dents but everyone's okay.

options.label: Find an alternate route
options.outcome: A longer path but no wildlife confrontation.

title: Caribou Herd Crossing
description: A herd of caribou is crossing ahead. Dozens of them, taking their time.

options.label: Stop and watch respectfully
options.outcome: Beautiful sight. The crew takes photos. Good for morale.

options.label: Radio ahead about the delay
options.outcome: Office appreciates the update. Lost an hour.

options.label: Try to pass slowly
options.outcome: The herd scatters. You save time but feel bad about it.

title: Worker Injury
description: A crew member slips on a log and goes down hard, clutching their ankle.

options.label: Apply first aid and continue
options.outcome: Bandaged up and back to light duty. They'll be sore for days.

options.label: Send them out for medical care
options.outcome: One truck heads to town. You're short-handed but it's the right call.

options.label: Have them rest in camp
options.outcome: They stay behind to heal. Might be fine tomorrow.

title: Bad Food
description: Half the crew woke up sick. Something in last night's dinner didn't agree.

options.label: Rest day for the sick
options.outcome: Those affected stay in camp. Work continues short-handed.

options.label: Push through anyway
options.outcome: Everyone works but productivity tanks. Some near-miss incidents.

options.label: Dispose of suspect food
options.outcome: You throw out everything questionable. Food stores depleted.

title: Chainsaw Accident
description: A saw kicks back. There's blood. This is serious.

options.label: Emergency first aid
options.outcome: The first aid kit gets heavy use. Bleeding stopped. Needs hospital.

options.label: Call helicopter medevac
options.outcome: Air ambulance arrives within the hour. They'll be okay. Expensive.

options.label: Drive to hospital (4 hours)
options.outcome: Someone drives fast. They make it. The crew is shaken.

title: Road Washed Out
description: The road ahead has collapsed into the creek. No way through.

options.label: Build a bypass
options.outcome: With shovels and chainsaws, you cut a rough detour. Takes all day.

options.label: Report and wait for road crew
options.outcome: A proper crew will fix it in 2 days. You're stuck here.

options.label: Try to ford the creek
options.outcome: A risky crossing. The truck makes it but scrapes bottom badly.

title: Questionable Bridge
description: The log bridge ahead looks... tired. Uncertain if it will hold the truck.

options.label: Test it on foot first
options.outcome: You walk across. It holds. Trucks go one at a time, very slowly.

options.label: Reinforce with fresh logs
options.outcome: An hour of cutting and the bridge is solid. Good work.

options.label: Find another crossing
options.outcome: A longer route around. Burns fuel but no bridge worries.

title: Landslide Debris
description: A slope has let go across the road. Trees, mud, and rock block the path.

options.label: Clear it with equipment
options.outcome: The skidder pushes debris aside. Hard on the machine.

options.label: Hand-clear a path
options.outcome: Everyone grabs tools. Exhausting but it works.

options.label: Turn back and report
options.outcome: Safety first. The office will coordinate geotechnical review.

title: Passing Supply Truck
description: Another crew's supply truck is passing through. They've got extras.

options.label: Trade for fuel
options.outcome: You swap some food for fuel. Fair trade.

options.label: Trade for food
options.outcome: Fresh supplies for the camp. Morale rises.

options.label: Just share news
options.outcome: Good conversation and road updates. Nothing more.

title: Supplies Lost
description: A load shifted on the rough road. A box of supplies bounced off somewhere.

options.label: Go back and search
options.outcome: Found most of it scattered along the road. Lost time.

options.label: Write it off and continue
options.outcome: Can't spare the time. That food is gone.

options.label: Secure remaining cargo
options.outcome: You stop to re-tie everything. No more losses.

title: Excellent Road Conditions
description: The road ahead is in great shape - recently graded and smooth.

options.label: Make up time!
options.outcome: The crew pushes forward quickly on the good road. Great progress!

options.label: Take it easy, save fuel
options.outcome: You cruise at low RPMs. Fuel gauge barely moves.

title: Abandoned Supply Cache
description: You spot an old camp with some leftover supplies. Looks usable.

options.label: Scavenge fuel and parts
options.outcome: A few jerry cans of diesel and some spare belts. Score!

options.label: Grab the food supplies
options.outcome: Canned goods and dried rations. Still good!

options.label: Take the first aid kit
options.outcome: A dusty but complete first aid kit. Could be a lifesaver.

title: Beautiful Sunset
description: The evening sky puts on a spectacular show. Everyone stops to watch.

options.label: Take a break and enjoy it
options.outcome: Moments like this remind everyone why they love this work.

options.label: Keep working but appreciate it
options.outcome: Even while busy, the beauty lifts spirits.

title: Helpful Locals
description: Some local First Nations members stop by with advice and coffee.

options.label: Share a meal and listen
options.outcome: They point out a shortcut and warn about a washout ahead.

options.label: Trade for supplies
options.outcome: They have smoked salmon to trade. Delicious and nutritious!

title: Equipment Running Smoothly
description: Everything is humming along perfectly today. Not a single mechanical issue.

options.label: Keep up the momentum
options.outcome: A productive day with no breakdowns. Equipment looks even better.

options.label: Do preventive maintenance
options.outcome: With things running well, you take time to check everything over.

title: Crew Member Threatens to Quit
description: Your most experienced faller corners you after dinner. He says he's had enough — the pay isn't worth the conditions. He wants out tomorrow morning.

options.label: Let them go
options.outcome: He packs up at dawn. The crew watches in silence. You're short-handed now.

options.label: Negotiate — offer a bonus and lighter duties
options.outcome: You promise extra pay and shorter shifts. He thinks it over...
options.failureOutcome: He takes the money but quits anyway two days later. Waste of cash.

options.label: Ignore it — he'll cool off
options.outcome: You shrug it off. But the rest of the crew noticed you didn't care. Tension rises.

title: Unauthorized Logging Nearby
description: You find fresh-cut stumps on the adjacent block. Someone's been logging here without permits — and it's not your crew.

options.label: Report it to the district office
options.outcome: You radio it in and file a report. The office appreciates it but sends an inspector, costing you a day of work.

options.label: Ignore it — not your problem
options.outcome: You keep working. But if someone finds out you saw it and said nothing...

options.label: Investigate quietly on your own
options.outcome: You poke around after hours. Fresh tire tracks lead to an unmarked road.

title: Confrontation at the Cutblock
description: A battered pickup rolls up to your camp. Two rough-looking guys step out — they know you've been snooping around their operation.

options.label: Stand your ground and tell them to leave
options.outcome: Tense standoff. They leave with threats but don't come back. Your crew is rattled.

options.label: Offer to look the other way
options.outcome: They leave you alone but you've compromised your integrity. Compliance risk if it comes out.

options.label: Call the authorities immediately
options.outcome: Conservation officers arrive within hours. The poachers scatter. You did the right thing.

title: Helicopter Available for Hire
description: A pilot radios in — his afternoon charter cancelled and he's offering discounted flight time. He could sling supplies to your next block or do an aerial recon.

options.label: Sling supplies ahead ($1500)
options.outcome: Equipment and fuel are waiting at the next block. Massive time savings.

options.label: Aerial recon of the route ($800)
options.outcome: From above you spot a washout, a good crossing, and the best path forward.

options.label: Save the money for emergencies
options.outcome: You wave him off. The budget stays intact but you wonder what could have been.

title: Your Best Worker Is Sick
description: Your most productive crew member wakes up feverish and pale. He insists he can work but he's clearly struggling.

options.label: Send them back to rest in camp
options.outcome: He sleeps all day. You lose your best producer but he should recover by tomorrow.

options.label: Let them do light duty
options.outcome: He putters around camp doing small tasks. Not much help, but he doesn't get worse. Probably.

options.label: Medicate and push through
options.outcome: You dose him up from the first aid kit. He works at half speed.

title: Safety Inspection Notice
description: A crackly radio message from district: a safety inspector will be visiting your operation in a few days. Better get things in order.

options.label: Spend the day prepping for inspection
options.outcome: You clean up the site, check PPE, organize paperwork. Lost a day of production but you'll be ready.

options.label: Do a quick tidy and keep working
options.outcome: A half-hearted cleanup. Things look okay-ish. You keep production moving.

options.label: Ignore it — we're always compliant
options.outcome: You trust your standards. The crew keeps working at full pace.

title: Safety Inspector Arrives
description: A white government truck pulls into camp. The inspector steps out with a clipboard and a serious expression.

options.label: Give them the full tour confidently
options.outcome: You walk them through every operation. They note a few minor issues but overall seem satisfied.

options.label: Be cooperative but let them lead
options.outcome: You answer questions honestly. They find what they find.

options.label: Distract them with coffee and stories
options.outcome: You're charming, but inspectors have seen this before. They dig deeper.

title: Suspicious Tracks Near Camp
description: Large canine tracks circle the camp perimeter. Too big for coyote. The food scraps from last night are gone.

options.label: Set up better food storage and carry on
options.outcome: You hang food higher and secure the garbage. Probably just a lone wolf passing through.

options.label: Post a night watch
options.outcome: Someone stays up with a flashlight. Tired crew tomorrow.

options.label: Report to wildlife office
options.outcome: They log it and tell you to be cautious. Standard advice.

title: Wolf Pack Sighting
description: At dusk, five grey wolves emerge from the treeline and watch your camp from the ridge. They're not afraid.

options.label: Light extra fires and make noise
options.outcome: The wolves linger but keep distance. An uneasy night. They're gone by morning.

options.label: Move camp to a more open area
options.outcome: A pain to relocate but worth it. No more wolf visits.

options.label: Carry on — wolves don't attack people
options.outcome: You're right, mostly. But the crew doesn't sleep well for days.

title: Radio Report: Incoming Weather System
description: The weather service broadcasts a warning: a major system is building to the west. Heavy rain and wind expected within 48 hours.

options.label: Push hard now to get ahead of it
options.outcome: The crew works double-time. Exhausting but you bank some progress before the storm.

options.label: Prepare camp for the storm
options.outcome: You batten down the hatches. Tarps, drainage ditches, extra firewood. Ready as you'll ever be.

options.label: Fall back to a safer position
options.outcome: You retreat to lower ground near the main road. Safe but far from the work site.

title: Major Storm Hits
description: The storm arrives with a vengeance. Trees bend sideways, rain comes in sheets, and the road turns to soup. No one's working today.

options.label: Hunker down and ride it out
options.outcome: A long, wet, miserable day. But everything holds together. Barely.

options.label: Evacuate to the main road
options.outcome: A harrowing drive through the storm. Everyone makes it out but the camp is left behind.

options.label: Use the downtime for equipment maintenance
options.outcome: Can't go anywhere, might as well wrench. Surprisingly productive shelter time.

title: Old-Growth Cedar Grove
description: Pushing through dense brush, you stumble into a cathedral-like grove of ancient cedars. Some of these trees are 500 years old. Sunlight filters through the canopy like stained glass.

options.label: Report the grove to the district
options.outcome: The office is grateful — this area needs protection. Your compliance record shines.

options.label: Mark it on the map and move on quietly
options.outcome: You GPS the location for your own records. A private treasure.

options.label: Let the crew take a break here
options.outcome: Everyone sits among the giants in awed silence. Someone says it's the best moment of the season.

title: Unmapped Creek Crossing
description: Your planned route crosses a creek that doesn't appear on any map. It's not huge, but it's definitely fish-bearing — you can see spawning beds.

options.label: Report it and reroute — proper protocol
options.outcome: The district is impressed by your diligence. A new buffer zone is established. Your route gets longer.

options.label: Cross carefully and note it for later
options.outcome: You ford it gently and mark it on the map. Should have reported it first, but you saved time.

options.label: Investigate the watershed
options.outcome: You spend time mapping the drainage. Valuable data for future planning.

title: Historical Survey Markers
description: Rusted iron pins and blazed trees — someone surveyed this area decades ago. The old markers could help confirm your boundary lines.

options.label: Record and photograph everything
options.outcome: Detailed documentation of the old survey. This data is gold for boundary disputes.

options.label: Use them to verify your current lines
options.outcome: Cross-referencing reveals your map is off by 40 meters. Good catch before you cut too far.

options.label: Note the location and keep moving
options.outcome: You GPS the pins and carry on. Useful info but not explored fully.

title: Abandoned Trapper's Cabin
description: Hidden in a stand of spruce, you find a weathered log cabin. The roof is mostly intact. Inside: a rusty stove, some old traps, and shelves of dusty supplies.

options.label: Use it as an emergency shelter
options.outcome: You patch the roof and clear it out. A solid backup shelter if weather turns nasty.

options.label: Salvage the supplies
options.outcome: Kerosene, rope, a hand axe, and some canned goods that are somehow still sealed. Lucky find.

options.label: Leave it undisturbed — could be heritage
options.outcome: You photograph it and report the location. The historical society is very interested.

title: High-Stakes Card Game
description: Tensions have been simmering. After dinner, a poker game gets heated. Two crew members are about to come to blows over a $200 pot.

options.label: Break it up and ban gambling
options.outcome: Order is restored but everyone resents the fun police. Quiet, sullen camp.

options.label: Mediate — split the pot and move on
options.outcome: A fair-enough solution. Nobody's happy but nobody's fighting.

options.label: Let them sort it out themselves
options.outcome: They scuffle. One gets a black eye. But somehow, they respect each other more after.

title: Radio Goes Dead
description: The camp radio crackles and dies mid-transmission. No signal, no spare parts. You're cut off from the outside.

options.label: Send someone to town for a replacement
options.outcome: A full day's drive, round trip. You're without comms until they return.

options.label: Try to fix it with what you have
options.outcome: Your most tech-savvy crew member tinkers for hours. Might work, might not.

options.label: Work without radio — check in when you can
options.outcome: Risky to be out of contact, but you keep production moving.

title: Fuel Contamination
description: The diesel smells wrong. Water got into the fuel barrel somehow. Your equipment sputters and stalls.

options.label: Drain and filter what you can
options.outcome: Tedious work. You save about half the fuel. Equipment runs rough for a day.

options.label: Dump it all and order fresh fuel
options.outcome: The safe choice but you're grounded until the delivery arrives.

options.label: Use it anyway — it's mostly diesel
options.outcome: The equipment runs, barely. Something is going to break sooner rather than later.

title: Competing Crew on Adjacent Block
description: Another logging crew has set up on the next block over. They're loud, fast, and their foreman keeps 'accidentally' encroaching on your boundary.

options.label: Confront the foreman directly
options.outcome: A heated but productive conversation. Boundaries are clarified. Uneasy truce.

options.label: Report the encroachment officially
options.outcome: Paperwork and phone calls. The district sends a surveyor. Takes days to resolve.

options.label: Challenge them to a production race
options.outcome: The crews push hard to outperform each other. Dangerous but motivating.

title: Urgent Call from Home
description: The sat phone rings. It's for one of your crew members — a family emergency back home. He's white-faced and shaking.

options.label: Let him go immediately
options.outcome: He drives out within the hour. The crew understands. You're short-handed but it's the right thing.

options.label: Ask him to stay until the shift ends
options.outcome: He stays but his mind is elsewhere. Dangerous distraction. The crew is uncomfortable.

options.label: Drive him to town yourself
options.outcome: You personally deliver him to the bus station. The crew respects the gesture enormously.

title: Raptor Nesting Area
description: An eagle nest sits in a massive Douglas fir right in your planned cut path. Two chicks are visible. This tree is protected by law.

options.label: Establish a buffer zone and reroute
options.outcome: You flag a 100-meter buffer. Adds distance but keeps you legal and ethical.

options.label: Report it and wait for wildlife assessment
options.outcome: A biologist will come out... eventually. Could be days.

options.label: Work around it as close as regulations allow
options.outcome: You cut right up to the legal minimum distance. Efficient but the eagles are stressed.

title: Missing Gear
description: Two chainsaws and a fuel can are missing from camp. Either someone took them or a very talented bear has new hobbies.

options.label: Search the area thoroughly
options.outcome: You find one saw tossed in the brush — dropped by whoever took it. The rest is gone.

options.label: Report theft to RCMP
options.outcome: Officers visit camp. Lots of questions, no answers. The crew feels violated.

options.label: Quietly increase camp security
options.outcome: You set up watches and lock everything down. No more incidents but the crew is on edge.

title: Spring Breakup Starting
description: The frost is coming out of the ground. Roads that were solid yesterday are turning to mush. The window for heavy equipment is closing fast.

options.label: Push hard now — run equipment day and night
options.outcome: You work around the clock while the ground still holds. Exhausting but productive.

options.label: Switch to lighter operations only
options.outcome: Hand-falling and foot-based work. Slow going but no road damage.

options.label: Shut down and wait for dry conditions
options.outcome: The responsible choice. Equipment stays parked. Could be a week or more.

title: Fresh Water Spring
description: A crew member finds a clear, cold spring bubbling up through the rocks. Crystal-clear water, probably safe to drink.

options.label: Fill up all the water containers
options.outcome: Fresh, cold water for the whole camp. Everyone's in better spirits.

options.label: Test it first, use it if clean
options.outcome: You use purification tablets just to be safe. Smart move — the water is perfect.

options.label: Note location and stick with camp water
options.outcome: Better safe than sorry. You GPS the spring for future reference.

title: Drone Flyover Opportunity
description: A forestry tech from another operation offers to fly his drone over your block during his lunch break. He's got a mapping camera.

options.label: Accept — map the whole block
options.outcome: Incredible aerial data. You can see drainage patterns, terrain features, and optimal routes from above.

options.label: Ask him to scout the road ahead
options.outcome: The drone reveals a major washout two kilometers ahead. You reroute and save a day.

options.label: Decline — don't need outside help
options.outcome: Your choice. The crew shrugs.

title: Something in the Dark
description: Something big crashes through the brush near camp at 2 AM. Flashlight beams catch reflective eyes before it disappears. Nobody's sure what it was.

options.label: Post a watch for the rest of the night
options.outcome: Nothing else happens, but the watchers are wrecked the next day.

options.label: Check tracks in the morning
options.outcome: Wolverine tracks. Impressive animal, not dangerous to the camp. Tensions ease.

options.label: Ignore it and go back to sleep
options.outcome: Some crew members sleep well, others don't close their eyes until dawn.

title: Swollen Creek Crossing
description: Recent rain has the creek running high. It's passable, maybe, but the current is strong and the bottom is uncertain.

options.label: Probe on foot, then drive across
options.outcome: You wade in waist-deep to test the bottom. Solid gravel. The trucks cross safely.

options.label: Wait for the water to drop
options.outcome: By afternoon it's lower. Safe crossing but half a day lost.

options.label: Build a temporary crossing
options.outcome: Logs and gravel create a rough bridge. Holds for one truck at a time.

title: First Nations Elder Visits
description: An elder from the local First Nations band arrives at camp. She says there are culturally modified trees nearby that need protection. She's calm but serious.

options.label: Stop work and walk the area with her
options.outcome: She shows you bark-stripped cedars with centuries of history. You adjust your cut plan together.

options.label: Acknowledge and promise to investigate later
options.outcome: She's skeptical but accepts your word. The relationship is strained.

options.label: Offer to hire her as a cultural monitor
options.outcome: She agrees. An added cost but the relationship and compliance benefits are enormous.

title: Natural Mineral Lick
description: You find a natural mineral lick heavily used by wildlife. Dozens of tracks — deer, elk, moose, bear — converge on this muddy depression.

options.label: Flag it and establish a buffer
options.outcome: The right thing to do. Wildlife officers will be pleased with the data.

options.label: Route around it quietly
options.outcome: You adjust the plan without formally reporting. Saves paperwork.

options.label: Set up a trail camera for fun
options.outcome: The crew checks the camera every morning like kids on Christmas. Incredible footage.

title: Hydraulic Line Burst
description: The loader's hydraulic line ruptures, spraying hot fluid everywhere. The machine drops its load and goes dead. There's oil soaking into the ground.

options.label: Contain the spill immediately
options.outcome: You deploy absorbent pads and dig a containment trench. Machine is down but the spill is managed.

options.label: Try to patch the line in the field
options.outcome: A temporary fix with hose clamps. It holds, barely. The machine runs at reduced pressure.

options.label: Shut everything down and call for a mechanic
options.outcome: Professional repair takes two days. Expensive but done right. You report the spill properly.

title: Perfect Falling Conditions
description: Cool air, no wind, dry ground, sharp saws. Every tree drops exactly where you aim. The crew is in a groove.

options.label: Ride the momentum — keep pushing
options.outcome: An exceptional day of production. The crew celebrates with extra rations at dinner.

options.label: Bank the good day and quit early
options.outcome: Ahead of schedule, so you call it at 3 PM. Rest and recovery.

title: Dense Morning Fog
description: You can't see twenty feet. The forest has vanished into white. Falling trees in this is asking for trouble.

options.label: Wait for it to burn off
options.outcome: By 10 AM the sun wins. Late start but safe.

options.label: Do ground work until it clears
options.outcome: Limbing, bucking, road clearing — no falling. Decent use of time.

options.label: Fall carefully with extra spotters
options.outcome: Slow and tense but productive. One close call that makes everyone sweat.

title: Supply Delivery Arrives Early
description: The supply truck shows up two days early. The driver says he had a cancellation and thought he'd swing by.

options.label: Full resupply — load up on everything
options.outcome: Fuel, food, parts — you take it all. Full stocks feel great.

options.label: Just grab what you're low on
options.outcome: A targeted resupply. Smart spending.

options.label: Send out your sick/injured crew member
options.outcome: The truck can take someone to town for proper medical care. Good opportunity.

title: Boundary Line Unclear
description: The flagging tape ends abruptly and the boundary marks don't match your map. If you cut the wrong trees, there'll be hell to pay.

options.label: Stop cutting and survey properly
options.outcome: You break out the GPS and compass. Takes half a day but the line is confirmed.

options.label: Use your best judgment and continue
options.outcome: You eyeball it based on terrain features. Could be right. Could be very wrong.

options.label: Call the office for clarification
options.outcome: They dig up the original survey data and talk you through it. Accurate but slow.

title: Apprentice Makes a Bad Cut
description: Your newest crew member drops a tree the wrong way. It hangs up in another tree — a dangerous 'widow maker' situation. He looks terrified.

options.label: Talk him through fixing it safely
options.outcome: Patient coaching. He learns, the tree comes down safely. Takes an hour but builds his skills.

options.label: Fix it yourself and send him to the truck
options.outcome: You handle it quickly but he's humiliated. Other crew members snicker.

options.label: Use the skidder to pull it down
options.outcome: Brute force solution. Works but burns fuel and risks equipment damage.

title: Forgotten Tool Cache
description: Under a deadfall, someone spots a weathered toolbox and a rolled-up tarp. Previous crew must have stashed them and forgot to come back.

options.label: Claim the tools for your crew
options.outcome: A spare chain, two files, a peavey, and some wedges. The tarp is still waterproof. Great find.

options.label: Try to find the original owner
options.outcome: You radio around. Turns out it's from a crew that disbanded last season. They tell you to keep it.

options.label: Leave it — could be someone's active cache
options.outcome: You mark the spot and leave everything. Respectful, if a bit wasteful.

title: Campfire Stories
description: After a long day, someone starts telling stories around the fire. One by one, the whole crew joins in. Laughter echoes through the trees.

options.label: Join in and share your own story
options.outcome: You tell the one about the time you got lost for three days. Everyone howls. Best night of the season.

options.label: Listen and let the crew bond
options.outcome: Sometimes the boss just needs to shut up and listen. The crew appreciates it.

options.label: Remind everyone about the early start tomorrow
options.outcome: The mood deflates instantly. Professional but joyless.
